options.label: D

question_text: Which of the following is/are correct for relation \mathbb{\mathbbfrac(R)} on the set of real numbers?
question_text_latex: Which of the following is/are correct for relation R on the set of real numbers?

options.label: A
options.text: (x, y) ∈ \mathbb{\mathbbfrac(R)} ⇔ 0 < |x| - |y| ≤ 1 is neither transitive nor symmetric.

options.label: B
options.text: (x, y) ∈ \mathbb{\mathbbfrac(R)} ⇔ 0 < |x - y| ≤ 1 is symmetric and transitive.

options.label: C
options.text: (x, y) ∈ \mathbb{\mathbbfrac(R)} ⇔ |x| - |y| ≤ 1 is reflexive but not symmetric.

options.label: D
options.text: (x, y) ∈ \mathbb{\mathbbfrac(R)} ⇔ |x - y| ≤ 1 is reflexive and symmetric.

options_latex.A: (x, y) \in R \iff 0 < |x| - |y| \leq 1 \text{ is neither transitive nor symmetric.}
options_latex.B: (x, y) \in R \iff 0 < |x - y| \leq 1 \text{ is symmetric and transitive.}
options_latex.C: (x, y) \in R \iff |x| - |y| \leq 1 \text{ is reflexive but not symmetric.}
options_latex.D: (x, y) \in R \iff |x - y| \leq 1 \text{ is reflexive and symmetric.}
